gestures.mdmPrompt: A person stands with feet shoulder-width apart, opens arms wide while enthusiastically explaining tree diagrams.
A tutor leans slightly forward, raises hands with palms up to introduce the example of 2 times 3.
A teacher points to an imaginary board, tilting head slightly while asking how many groups we have.
A tutor points downwards with the right hand, nodding encouragingly while asking about the number under each group.
A person steps slightly forward, opens both hands invitingly while asking if students can use a tree diagram for 4 times 2.
A tutor stands confidently, moves both hands to the side, emphasizing clarity in organizing groups and items.
A teacher steps forward slightly, raises both hands up with excitement while introducing the new example of 3 times 4.
A tutor points forward with their right hand while actively engaging students, asking how many groups we have.
A teacher points down with the right hand, looks at students encouragingly while asking for the number under each group.
A person stands firmly, opens both hands wide, while inviting students to try the original problem about using a tree diagram.
